results_with_obs.note: Rounds 1-3 had no saved observation data; rounds 4-6 used real obs from seeds 0-2

results_oracle.note: Upper bound: uses round ground truth as perfect observations

prob_floor_experiment.verdict: floor 0.02 is strictly worse across all 6 rounds; keep 0.01

observations_impact.note: Observations help most on rounds where hist-only transitions are a poor fit (round 6: +19.2 pts)

per_class_weaknesses.settlement: KL 0.44-1.00, score 4.9-27.5. Worst class by far. 5-7% entropy share but dominates error.
per_class_weaknesses.port: KL 0.29-0.91, score 6.5-42.0. Few cells but high KL. Very volatile across rounds.
per_class_weaknesses.empty: KL 0.05-0.23, score 50.9-85.2. Largest class (65-69% entropy). Acceptable but varies.
per_class_weaknesses.forest: KL 0.05-0.28, score 43.5-84.1. Second largest class. Tracks empty pattern.
per_class_weaknesses.mountain: KL 0.051, score 85.7. Static terrain, essentially solved.

round3_anomaly.diagnosis: Settlement KL=1.00 (score 4.9) and port KL=0.91 (score 6.5) destroyed round 3. Dynamics differ radically from other rounds. Even oracle only reaches 41.7.
round3_anomaly.implication: Hidden parameters vary widely between rounds. Cross-round transition transfer has limits.

top_insight: Observations boost score by +8.6 pts avg (up to +19 on outlier rounds); settlement prediction (KL~0.5) remains the primary bottleneck: 5% of cells but drives most error. Floor 0.02 hurts uniformly (-2.0 pts). Next improvement should target settlement/port class modeling specifically.
